cityDNA.coreFantasy: A shade-spectral university city where lecture courts, hidden markets, archive towers, slums, tenements, veiled courts, and shadeglass thresholds make visibility political.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Gloamreach is not a ghost town; it is a living spectral city where being seen, recorded, hidden, housed, or ignored is civic power.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Gloamreach turns knowledge, secrecy, archive memory, hidden trade, poverty, domestic persistence, mist, shadeglass, and threshold law into one civic system of partial visibility.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: White limestone, moonstone masonry, shadeglass, black slate roofs, gothic tracery, blue lamps, mist-veiled glass, archive towers, lecture halls, black-market arcades, scrap markets, patched tenements, laundry wells, and spectral citizens.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A shade-spectral civilization of professors, students, copyists, court officials, brokers, scrap dealers, tenement families, and under-vault runners stratified by access to records, lectures, vaults, and clean visibility.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Mist veils, shadeglass, moonstone, blue lamps, lecture towers, archive seals, black-market vaults, patched cloth, laundry wells, threshold arches, and ghostly citizens symbolize controlled revelation.

canonLocks: Gloamreach is a mature shade-spectral civic city, not abandoned haunted scenery.
The Wonder is the Shadeglass Whisper Court of the Veiled Threshold.
The Beacon is the Lecture Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Mist Veils Veiled High Court.
The five primary districts are University / Lyceum, Underworld / Hidden Economy, Records / Archives, Slums / Informal Settlement, and Low Residential.

The uploaded district anchors include University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall Beacon, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, and Laundry Well Nook.
provisionalCanon: Gloamreach's highest civic virtue is discretion.
The central tension is mystery versus exclusion.
Low residential and slum anchors make visibility a class issue, not only a magical one.
rejectedReads: Gloamreach is a generic ghost town.
Gloamreach is empty ruin horror rather than inhabited spectral civic life.
Scholarship, hidden economy, archives, slums, and tenements are separate aesthetics rather than one class-stratified system.
cautionFlags: Write spectral citizens as full people with jobs, homes, hunger, status, education, and errands.
Keep scholarship and poverty in the same visual frame.
Use thresholds, veils, seals, mist, and shadeglass as social mechanics, not decoration.
Preserve exact slum and low-residential sidecar anchors so the city does not collapse into pure university/gothic mood.

environment.environmentalLogic: Mist, shadow, height, and buried space are civic materials. Gloamreach organizes gradients of visibility: what is taught, recorded, whispered, hidden below, and forced into view by poverty.

skyline: Shadeglass Whisper Court of the Veiled Threshold as far wonder anchor
Mist Veils Veiled High Court as central authority
Lecture Hall Beacon as scholarly signal landmark
domed lecture halls, observatory towers, gothic spires, tenement courts, and fog terraces layered across pale slopes

civilization.people: Citizens are shade spectral folk: translucent, veiled, pale-lit, quiet-bodied, socially varied, and fully civic rather than merely haunting the city.
civilization.governance: Authority is veiled, archival, and knowledge-based. The High Court governs through sealed archives, controlled thresholds, disclosure rulings, lecture legitimacy, and permitted opacity.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is threshold-oriented and spectral, carried through veils, shadeglass, whisper rites, memory sealing, star navigation, and ceremonies of controlled disclosure.

streets.appearance: Pale, wet, vertical, and half-seen: lecture halls, moonstone domes, blue lamps, observatory instruments, archive stairs, black-market thresholds, scrap stalls, patched tenements, and laundry wells share the mist.
streets.movement: Movement follows stepped lecture plazas, archive stairs, hidden vault routes, market arcades, tenement alleys, laundry courtyards, low bridges, and threshold courts.
streets.lighting: Day is overcast silver over moonstone and pale spectral bodies; night is blue-lit, candle-dotted, archive-windowed, black-market-lanterned, and shadeglass-reflective.
streets.weatherEffects: Fog, drizzle, cold mist, and low cloud reduce certainty, blur class boundaries visually, and make every threshold feel socially charged.
streets.dayCycle: Morning begins with lectures, archive desks, copyists, and sealed records; afternoon stirs markets, vault negotiations, scrap repair, and petitions.
streets.nightCycle: Night shifts toward black-market lanterns, tenement lamps, laundry gossip, archive windows, vault traffic, and shadeglass reflections.

housing.wealthy: Pale stone scholar apartments and court-adjacent rooms with moonstone windows, blue lamps, private studies, archive access, and veiled balconies.
housing.middle: Rooms over bookshops, copyist homes, lecture-court apartments, modest archive flats, narrow balconies, shared lamps, and damp stone stairs.
housing.poor: Patched tenements, timber longhouses, laundry-well courtyards, scrap-market rooms, damp blankets, cheap candles, shared water, and fog-leaking shutters.

districts.name: University / Lyceum
districts.civicPurpose: Public instruction, scholarly authority, observatory orientation, and Beacon-centered signal interpretation.
districts.visualIdentity: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall Beacon, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, moonstone domes, white limestone, and blue lamps.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Scholarly, pale, anxious, and publicly authoritative.

districts.name: Underworld / Hidden Economy
districts.civicPurpose: Hidden commerce, forbidden records, vault debts, rumor circulation, and tolerated civic shadow.
districts.visualIdentity: Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, coded stalls, gothic shadows, blue-black lanterns, and hidden stairs.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Official memory, legal record, copied truth, sealed archives, and public legibility.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, archive stairs, sealed records, pale stone, and blue archive windows.

districts.name: Slums / Informal Settlement
districts.civicPurpose: Survival trade, repair culture, informal care, and life in the gaps between official systems.
districts.visualIdentity: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, makeshift stalls, wet lanes, and repaired goods.

districts.name: Low Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Ordinary spectral domesticity, rent pressure, neighborhood gossip, shared water, and modest continuity.
districts.visualIdentity: Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook, timber longhouses, modest markets, clotheslines, damp stone, and blue courtyard light.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Domestic, cramped, persistent, and socially watchful.

wonder.name: Shadeglass Whisper Court of the Veiled Threshold
wonder.symbolicRole: The threshold between seen and unseen, spoken and withheld, living and spectral, official and whispered.
wonder.visualRole: Vast shadeglass court of veiled arches, reflective dark glass, pale stone, blue spectral light, misty terraces, whispering galleries, and threshold geometry.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale civic site for threshold ceremonies, oaths of disclosure, veiled court observances, scholarly convocations, whisper rites, and memory sealing.
storyHooks: A whisper rite reveals that the city protects some truths by burying others.

beacon.name: Lecture Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Knowledge illuminating danger unevenly through mist.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-lit signal landmark rising from the University / Lyceum district among lecture courts, archive towers, moonstone, observatory instruments, and wet plazas.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates lecture musters, public warnings, scholarly announcements, student gatherings, and archive alerts.
storyHooks: Whoever explains the Beacon first controls what the city believes it means.

seatOfPower.name: Mist Veils Veiled High Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Governance through controlled visibility and authorized truth.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central high court of pale stone, mist veils, black banners, sealed archive wings, audience terraces, command platforms, blue lamps, and gothic approaches.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Handles audiences, sealed hearings, archive rulings, public petitions, threshold decrees, scholarly appointments, and disclosure ceremonies.
storyHooks: A High Court decree decides when a truth is allowed to exist in public.

majorLandmarks.name: Black Market Arcade
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: The hidden economy the city denies and depends on.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Coded stalls, vault doors, black-market lanterns, mist-veiled arches, and subterranean stairs.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Contraband knowledge, vault debt, relic trade, informal supply, and rumor exchange.
storyHooks: A broker sells the missing half of an official archive record.

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Official memory made vertical and selective.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Tall pale archive with gold seals, blue windows, copyist halls, sealed stairs, and record balconies.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Stores records, petitions, legal copies, civic history, and controlled memory.
storyHooks: A gold-sealed copy contradicts the version taught in the lecture hall.

majorLandmarks.name: Scrap Market
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Patched stalls, scrap piles, lantern monument, damp lanes, and repaired tools.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Informal repair, survival commerce, neighborhood exchange, and overlooked evidence.
storyHooks: A repair mark proves what the archive refuses to name.

majorLandmarks.name: Tenement Court
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Ordinary domestic persistence in a city of controlled visibility.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Low residential court with laundry well, lantern monument, patched timber, clotheslines, damp steps, and pale spectral families.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Housing, shared water, gossip, tenancy pressure, and daily continuity.
storyHooks: A laundry-well complaint identifies a truth no court has authorized.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall Beacon, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, professors, blue lamps, and wet lecture plazas.
S2 must show Underworld / Hidden Economy Civic Hall, Black Market Arcade, Mist Veils Monument, Understreet Vault Nook, coded stalls, brokers, and vault doors.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, archive stairs, sealed records, and copyists.
S4 must show Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, repair stalls, scrap dealers, and informal residents.
S5 must show Low Residential Civic Hall, Tenement Court, Low Residential Lantern Monument, Laundry Well Nook, families, laundry lines, and shared stairs.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show pale moonstone and shadeglass terraces with lecture halls, archive towers, black-market thresholds, scrap markets, tenement courts, and laundry wells in the same misted urban system.
The Shadeglass Whisper Court should be the wonder-scale threshold anchor; the Mist Veils Veiled High Court should be central authority; the Lecture Hall Beacon should be the scholarly signal.
requiredVisualElements: Shadeglass Whisper Court of the Veiled Threshold
elementsToAvoid: pure university city with no poverty
